rationale: All empirical checks pass (validate exits 0 with only the lifecycle-binding warning), scope is exactly five files, and fences are clean. However, the ADR and all three skill surfaces overclaim the shipped gate: they universally promise refusal for any closed bead lacking proof/closed without complete, although runOrphanObligationGate cannot detect an already raw-git-merged bead with no durable obligation. The ADR later admits this residual, making its earlier universal claim contradictory; the skills do not disclose it. AC10 is also gameable: claimsLifecyclePackage recognizes only lifecycle-prefixed patterns. A second domain may claim internal/**, which the repository's real GlobMatch treats as owning lifecycle files, yet the test still reports workflow as the sole claimant.
concrete_changes_required: Qualify ADR lines 160-166 and the sentence in all three skill surfaces beginning '`mindspec impl approve` REFUSES' so they claim only detectable unmerged merge candidates or durable uncovered obligations; preserve the disclosed raw-git-merged/no-obligation residual. Avoid promising unconditional success after recovery because other approval gates may still refuse.

Replace claimsLifecyclePackage's string-prefix heuristic with ownership matching that detects every manifest glob overlapping actual internal/lifecycle files (including broader globs such as internal/**), and add RED tests for a broader second-domain claim as well as exact duplicate and removed-workflow cases.

findings.summary: go build, package tests, focused ownership test, gofmt, vet, golangci-lint, and validate all exited 0. AC9 counts: ADR 5; each skill surface 2. Validate emitted one lifecycle-binding WARN only.

Exact overclaim: ADR says 'REFUSES ... while any closed bead ... lacks proof of panel settlement'; skills say 'when any closed bead ... was closed without mindspec complete'. The shipped gate misses an already raw-git-merged bead with no durable obligation, as the ADR's own residual later acknowledges.

The test parses all four manifests and asserts cardinality plus workflow identity, but its matcher ignores broader owning globs such as internal/**. Thus it can pass while a second domain owns every internal/lifecycle file under the production GlobMatch semantics.
Diff is limited to the ADR, three skill surfaces, and ownership test; no OWNERSHIP.yaml or Bead-1/2 code changed. BranchExistsE/show-ref search returned zero hits.
